topPlaces.name: Maladevi Temple
topPlaces.description: Maladevi Temple is a Jain temple located in Gyaraspur town of Vidisha in state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is a rock-cut temple built in Gurjara Pratihara style and dates back to the 850–900 CE worship site.

topPlaces.name: Jain temples, Vidisha
topPlaces.description: Vidisha is considered to be Puranakshetras Jain tirtha. The Jain temples are located in Vidisha district in state of Madhya Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Gyaraspur
topPlaces.description: Gyaraspur is a town in the Vidisha district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It is also the headquarters of a tehsil of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Muhammadgarh, India
topPlaces.description: Mohammadgarh or Muhammadgarh is a town in Gyaraspur tehsil, Vidisha district, Bhopal division of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is located at an altitude of 432 m above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Mohammadgarh State
topPlaces.description: Mohammadgarh State, also spelt as 'Muhammadgarh', was a former princely state in Central India, under the Bhopal Agency during the British Raj. It was situated in the Malwa Plateau.

items.note: Maladevi Temple is a Jain temple located in Gyaraspur town of Vidisha in state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is a rock-cut temple built in Gurjara Pratihara style and dates back to the 850–900 CE worship site.
Vidisha is considered to be Puranakshetras Jain tirtha. The Jain temples are located in Vidisha district in state of Madhya Pradesh, India.
Gyaraspur is a town in the Vidisha district, Madhya Pradesh, India. It is also the headquarters of a tehsil of the same name.
Mohammadgarh or Muhammadgarh is a town in Gyaraspur tehsil, Vidisha district, Bhopal division of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is located at an altitude of 432 m above sea level.
Mohammadgarh State, also spelt as 'Muhammadgarh', was a former princely state in Central India, under the Bhopal Agency during the British Raj. It was situated in the Malwa Plateau.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Raja Bhoj Airport, Bhopal (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (85 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur?
faq.a: Stays in Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bajramath Temple, Gyaraspur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Maladevi Temple, Jain temples, Vidisha, Gyaraspur, Muhammadgarh, India, Mohammadgarh State.
